meta.title: Matthew Scott Wixted — Wixted Line
meta.summary: From Tipperary blacksmiths and Lambeth, London to Corning & Rochester, New York — then California.

migrations.label: Thomas James Wixted arrives in America

generations.name: Matthew Scott Wixted
generations.detail: Born in Whittier, CA. Center of this family tree — four siblings in the Kevin & Angela Wixted family.

generations.name: Kevin Paul Wixted
generations.detail: Born in Rochester, New York. Married Angela Maxine Amor, 29 May 1993 (div. Dec 2005). Raised the Whittier, CA generation.

generations.name: Angela Maxine Amor
generations.detail: Born in Long Beach, CA. The Amor family branch connects Matthew to California roots on his mother's side.

generations.name: Bruce John Wixted
generations.detail: Born in Rochester, NY. With wife Evelyn Ruth Jones, moved the family to Phoenix, AZ (1963) and Orange County, CA (1971). Son of John Leroy Wixted; carried the Wixted name west.

generations.name: Evelyn Ruth Jones
generations.detail: Rochester librarian (SSN 112-24-1440). Raised four children before the 1963 migration west with Bruce Wixted.

generations.name: John Leroy Wixted
generations.detail: Born in Corning, raised in Rochester. Census records from 1910 Avon through 1930 Rochester. Father of Bruce John Wixted; Steuben County obituary index confirms b. 1900, d. 1993.

generations.name: Henry Joseph Wixted
generations.detail: Corning-born Wixted. Erie Railroad era — a 1915 newspaper noted an electrical accident. Census records trace him from age 4 (1870) through 1880 in Corning.

generations.name: Henry Wixted
generations.detail: Documented across Corning censuses 1855–1880. Son of Thomas James Wixted and Mary Hogan, who emigrated from Lambeth, London.

generations.name: Thomas James Wixted
generations.detail: Born 1796, died Lambeth, London 1873. Workbook patriarch of the American Wixted line. Research links to Wickstead/Wixsted gentry of Cheshire, England.

generations.name: Mary Hogan
generations.detail: Irish-born wife of Thomas James Wixted. Together they bridge the London emigration and the Corning, NY settlement.

generations.name: Wixted of Tipperary
generations.detail: The Wixted name is established in Ireland since the 1600s, chiefly Tipperary. WikiTree documents John Wixted's 1815 baptism at Englishtown — blacksmith trade passed father to son.

researchNotes: Corning censuses (1855–1930) anchor the NY Wixted line across five generations.
Steuben County obituary index lists John L, Hazel M, Raymond J, and Vincent D Wixted — Corning & Bath burials.
John Leroy Wixted (1900–1993) links the Rochester generation to Corning-born Henry Joseph Wixted.
Bruce & Evelyn's 1963 Rochester→Phoenix→Orange County migration explains Matthew's California roots.
Thomas James Wixted (1796) is the workbook gateway to English Wickstead pedigrees and Irish Tipperary records.
